headline: The White House reversed its two-week restriction on Anthropic's most advanced models while agent-training techniques broke out across the field.
body: The clearest policy shift this month came from Washington, where the White House imposed and then dropped export controls on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models, with Anthropic set to restore Fable access beginning July 1. On the technical side, agent-oriented methods dominated the rotation: trajectory data, agent skills, behavioral auditing, ablation, and masking all led and broke out together, alongside new entrants Gradient Staleness, HNSW, and deterministic behavior. The Model Context Protocol featured heavily in new items, with Google Cloud framing it against traditional APIs and building enterprise agentic platforms on it. Model evaluation also drew attention as Sonnet 5 landed to reviews claiming it underperforms GLM-5.2 and Opus at higher cost.

cited_items.title: White House drops restrictions on Anthropic AI models after two-week ban - The Washington Post
